name: filter_condition sprint_value uses Go %g format for boundary integers
description: Locks behavior at the to_chars vs Go %g boundary (100000 / 1000000 / 1000001). Go's filter_condition compares Sprintf("%v", item) == Sprintf("%v", value); for float64 this is %g. C++'s sprint_value originally used std::to_chars shortest mode which switches to scientific at smaller magnitudes, so a number value 100000 stringifies as "1e+05" and fails to match the string item "100000". Reference: P0-2 in `.llmdoc-tmp/cpp-runtime-parity-audit/03-runtime.md`.

cases.name: number 100000 must match string "100000" via Go %g
